surface: rgoc CLI + generated Go artifacts + local HTTP probe

isolation.notes: All repo commands ran through nix develop -c ... with DUNE_BUILD_DIR isolated under the evidence directory; the generated server bound only to 127.0.0.1:3111.

toolsUsed: nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR=... dune exec rgoc -- ... -o ...

assertions.title: Stdlib call sites use snake_case while stdlib type names stay PascalCase

steps.action: Compile a representative fixture that imports net::http, registers mux.handle_func, calls http::new_serve_mux/http::listen_and_serve, and names http::Request/http::ResponseWriter/http::ServeMux in source.
steps.expected: rgoc exits 0 and writes Go output.

steps.action: Inspect the generated Go and run downstream Go checks.
steps.expected: Generated Go lowers to http.ListenAndServe, http.NewServeMux, mux.HandleFunc, *http.Request, and http.ResponseWriter; gofmt -d, go build, and go vet all succeed.
steps.observed: Generated Go imports "net/http" and contains http.ListenAndServe, http.NewServeMux, mux.HandleFunc, *http.Request, http.ResponseWriter, r.Method, r.FormValue, w.WriteHeader, and w.Write; gofmt -d, go build, and go vet all exited 0.

commands: nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR=<evidence>/dune-build dune exec rgoc -- <evidence>/fixtures/stdlib_runtime_positive.rg -o <evidence>/outputs/stdlib_runtime_positive.go

assertions.title: Stdlib receiver/member naming works in real handler code

steps.action: Build the generated Go server, start it on 127.0.0.1:3111, and probe GET /items.
steps.expected: A live request reaches the handler and proves req.method + w.write_header/w.write execute successfully.
steps.observed: GET /items returned HTTP 200 with body `items method=GET`.

steps.action: Probe POST /items with form data and then negative method/data paths.
steps.expected: POST exercises req.form_value and returns a response derived from the submitted form data; malformed POST and DELETE should still hit the same handler and produce stable 400/405 responses.
steps.observed: POST /items with `name=alice` returned HTTP 201 with `created alice via POST`; POST without name returned HTTP 400 with `missing name via POST`; DELETE /items returned HTTP 405 with `unsupported DELETE`.

evidence.network: GET /items -> 200; POST /items name=alice -> 201; POST /items missing name -> 400; DELETE /items -> 405

steps.action: Compile representative fixtures that use mux.HandleFunc, r.FormValue, r.Method, w.WriteHeader, and w.Write.
steps.expected: Each compile fails with a clear wrong-case diagnostic and no generated Go file is written.
steps.observed: All five fixtures exited 1 with direct `wrong case` diagnostics naming the required snake_case spelling; no requested output files were created.

commands: nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR=<evidence>/dune-build dune exec rgoc -- <evidence>/fixtures/wrong_case_handle_func.rg -o <evidence>/outputs/wrong_case_handle_func.go
nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR=<evidence>/dune-build dune exec rgoc -- <evidence>/fixtures/wrong_case_form_value.rg -o <evidence>/outputs/wrong_case_form_value.go
nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR=<evidence>/dune-build dune exec rgoc -- <evidence>/fixtures/wrong_case_method.rg -o <evidence>/outputs/wrong_case_method.go
nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR=<evidence>/dune-build dune exec rgoc -- <evidence>/fixtures/wrong_case_write_header.rg -o <evidence>/outputs/wrong_case_write_header.go
nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR=<evidence>/dune-build dune exec rgoc -- <evidence>/fixtures/wrong_case_write.rg -o <evidence>/outputs/wrong_case_write.go

assertions.title: Import, naming, and stdlib type usage compose coherently in one program

steps.action: Compile a single program that imports use net::http and combines http::listen_and_serve, http::new_serve_mux, mux.handle_func, and source-level http::Request/http::ResponseWriter/http::ServeMux usage.
steps.expected: Compilation succeeds and generated Go shows the expected lowered stdlib spellings while preserving the source-level PascalCase type names contract.
steps.observed: The combined fixture compiled successfully, built cleanly, and generated Go contains import "net/http" together with http.ListenAndServe, http.NewServeMux, mux.HandleFunc, *http.Request, and http.ResponseWriter.

commands: nix develop -c env DUNE_BUILD_DIR=<evidence>/dune-build dune exec rgoc -- <evidence>/fixtures/stdlib_runtime_positive.rg -o <evidence>/outputs/stdlib_runtime_positive.go

frictions.description: Each nix develop invocation prints the flake shell banner, so success logs are non-empty even when gofmt -d and go vet are clean.
frictions.resolution: Used saved logs plus generated-Go inspection; the gofmt/vet logs contain only the shell banner and no diff or diagnostic output.

frictions.description: curl -i includes progress-meter lines in the captured response files, which adds noise ahead of the HTTP status/body evidence.
frictions.resolution: Kept the full response captures and evaluated the trailing HTTP status lines and bodies for each request.

summary: Tested 4 assigned assertions on the rgoc CLI/generated-Go/local-HTTP surface: VAL-STDLIB-001 passed, VAL-STDLIB-003 passed, VAL-STDLIB-004 passed, and VAL-CROSS-001 passed. The representative fixture lowered net/http names correctly, downstream gofmt/go build/go vet all succeeded, live requests exercised the handler code paths, and all Go-cased receiver-member negatives failed with direct wrong-case diagnostics and no output files.
